# DDx CLI Makefile

# Build variables
VERSION ?= $(shell git describe --tags --always --dirty)
COMMIT ?= $(shell git rev-parse --short HEAD)
DATE ?= $(shell date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)
LDFLAGS = -ldflags "-X main.version=${VERSION} -X main.commit=${COMMIT} -X main.date=${DATE}"

# Build directory
BUILD_DIR = build

# Platforms to build for
PLATFORMS = \
	darwin/amd64 \
	darwin/arm64 \
	linux/amd64 \
	linux/arm64 \
	windows/amd64

.PHONY: all build clean test lint deps help obsidian-migrate obsidian-validate obsidian-test obsidian-dry-run obsidian-nav obsidian-revert bead-schema skill-schema gendocs frontend-build frontend-rebuild frontend-verify copy-skills eval-skill bench-graphql

# Default target
all: clean deps test build

# Build SvelteKit frontend (required before Go build for //go:embed).
# Skip rebuild when no source file is newer than the last build output.
FRONTEND_SRC := $(shell find internal/server/frontend/src internal/server/frontend/static -type f 2>/dev/null)
FRONTEND_STAMP := internal/server/frontend/build/index.html

frontend-build: $(FRONTEND_STAMP)

$(FRONTEND_STAMP): $(FRONTEND_SRC) internal/server/frontend/package.json internal/server/frontend/svelte.config.js internal/server/frontend/vite.config.ts
	@echo "Building SvelteKit frontend..."
	cd internal/server/frontend && bun install --frozen-lockfile && bun run build
	@$(MAKE) --no-print-directory frontend-verify

# Force a frontend rebuild regardless of timestamps
frontend-rebuild:
	@echo "Rebuilding SvelteKit frontend (forced)..."
	cd internal/server/frontend && bun install --frozen-lockfile && bun run build
	@$(MAKE) --no-print-directory frontend-verify

# Verify the embedded frontend bundle is internally consistent: every
# /_app/* asset referenced by index.html must exist on disk. Catches the
# class of bug where index.html and the chunk dir get out of sync (partial
# build, file restore, manual edit) and go:embed silently bakes in a
# broken bundle that 404s every JS chunk to the SPA shell.
frontend-verify:
	@echo "Verifying embedded frontend bundle integrity..."
	@cd internal/server/frontend/build && \
	missing=0; \
	for ref in $$(grep -hoE '"/_app/[^"]+"' index.html | tr -d '"' | sort -u); do \
		path=".$$ref"; \
		if [ ! -f "$$path" ]; then \
			echo "  MISSING: $$ref"; \
			missing=$$((missing+1)); \
		fi; \
	done; \
	if [ $$missing -gt 0 ]; then \
		echo "frontend-verify: $$missing referenced asset(s) missing — run 'make frontend-rebuild'"; \

133	# Run linter
134	lint:
135		@echo "Running linter..."
136		@if command -v golangci-lint >/dev/null 2>&1; then \
137			golangci-lint run; \
138		else \
139			echo "golangci-lint not found, running go vet instead"; \
140			go vet ./...; \
141		fi
142	
143	# Install locally for development
144	install: build
145		@echo "Installing DDx locally..."
146		cp $(BUILD_DIR)/ddx $(HOME)/.local/bin/ddx
147		@echo "DDx installed to ~/.local/bin/ddx"
148	
149	# Clean build artifacts

1	// Package routinglint implements the FEAT-006 routing-cleanup lint
2	// rule that mechanically forbids reintroduction of DDx-side
3	// "compensating routing" logic that was deleted with prejudice in
4	// ddx-3bd7396a.
5	//
6	// The parent epic ddx-fdd3ea36 §AC#7 says:
7	//
8	//	"Search DDx for code that re-implements upstream routing
9	//	concerns: allow-list checks, exact-pin filtering,
10	//	profile-to-preference mapping, provider cost scoring. Must be
11	//	absent on the default path. ... Dead-code detector run in CI
12	//	catches orphans."
13	//
14	// Parent-epic AC#7 originally expected the helpers to remain present,
15	// reachable only via --escalate. The follow-up bead ddx-3bd7396a
16	// instead deleted them entirely, so this analyzer enforces the
17	// stricter invariant: the listed compensating-routing identifiers and
18	// flag literals must not reappear anywhere in cli/ Go source. If they
19	// do, the cleanup has regressed and CI fails.
20	//
21	// Forbidden identifiers (exact name match):
22	//
23	//	ResolveProfileLadder, ResolveTierModelRef, AdaptiveMinTier,
24	//	workersByHarness, ResolveProfileLadderCallCount.
25	//
26	// Forbidden string literals (anywhere in cli/ Go source):
27	//
28	//	"--escalate", "--override-model",
29	//	"profile_ladders", "model_overrides",
30	//	"agent.routing.profile_ladders",
31	//	"agent.routing.model_overrides".
32	//
33	// String-literal occurrences inside this analyzer's own package and
34	// inside testdata fixtures are exempt.
35	//
36	// A specific occurrence may be intentionally exempted (e.g. the
37	// migration code that rejects a config containing
38	// agent.routing.profile_ladders has to name the literal key) by
39	// adding a
40	//
41	//	// routinglint:legacy-rejection reason="..."
42	//
43	// comment on the same line as the offending node, or on a comment
44	// line directly above it. The reason= clause must be present and
45	// non-empty.
46	package routinglint
47	
48	import (
49		"go/ast"
50		"go/token"
51		"regexp"
52		"strings"
53	
54		"golang.org/x/tools/go/analysis"
55		"golang.org/x/tools/go/analysis/passes/inspect"
56		"golang.org/x/tools/go/ast/inspector"
57	)
58	
59	const exemptionAnnotation = "routinglint:legacy-rejection"
60	
61	var exemptionRE = regexp.MustCompile(`routinglint:legacy-rejection\s+reason="[^"]+"`)
62	
63	// Analyzer is the FEAT-006 routing-cleanup analyzer.
64	var Analyzer = &analysis.Analyzer{
65		Name:     "routinglint",
66		Doc:      "flags reintroduction of compensating DDx-side routing helpers and flags retired by FEAT-006 / ddx-3bd7396a",
67		Requires: []*analysis.Analyzer{inspect.Analyzer},
68		Run:      run,
69	}
70	
71	// forbiddenIdents is the closed list of compensating-routing
72	// identifiers retired by ddx-3bd7396a. Reintroduction is a CI failure.
73	var forbiddenIdents = map[string]string{
74		"ResolveProfileLadder":          "tier-ladder resolver retired by ddx-3bd7396a — DDx must not re-implement profile-to-preference mapping",
75		"ResolveTierModelRef":           "tier-model resolver retired by ddx-3bd7396a — DDx must not re-implement exact-pin filtering",
76		"ResolveProfileLadderCallCount": "test seam for the retired tier-ladder resolver — also retired by ddx-3bd7396a",
77		"AdaptiveMinTier":               "adaptive-min-tier promotion logic retired by ddx-3bd7396a",
78		"workersByHarness":              "escalation worker grouping helper retired by ddx-3bd7396a",
79		"queueByHarness":                "using harness as a queue-selection criterion violates the passthrough envelope contract (ddx-20047dd5)",
80		"retryByPassthrough":            "using passthrough values in retry policy violates the passthrough envelope contract (ddx-20047dd5)",
81		"endpointByPassthrough":         "using passthrough values in endpoint selection violates the passthrough envelope contract (ddx-20047dd5)",
82		"catalogThresholdForHarness":    "using harness in catalog threshold evaluation violates the passthrough envelope contract (ddx-20047dd5)",
83	}
84	
85	// forbiddenLiterals is the closed list of CLI flag and config-key
86	// strings retired by ddx-3bd7396a. They must not appear as Go string
87	// literals anywhere in cli/.
88	var forbiddenLiterals = map[string]string{
89		"--escalate":                    "--escalate flag retired by ddx-3bd7396a — escalation is not a DDx-side concern",
90		"--override-model":              "--override-model flag retired by ddx-3bd7396a — model overrides go through --model on the default path",
91		"profile_ladders":               "agent.routing.profile_ladders config key retired by ddx-3bd7396a",
92		"model_overrides":               "agent.routing.model_overrides config key retired by ddx-3bd7396a",
93		"agent.routing.profile_ladders": "agent.routing.profile_ladders config key retired by ddx-3bd7396a",
94		"agent.routing.model_overrides": "agent.routing.model_overrides config key retired by ddx-3bd7396a",
95	}
96	
97	// selfPkgFragments lists path fragments that mark this analyzer's own
98	// package or its testdata fixtures. Diagnostics from those packages
99	// are suppressed because the closed-list constants above legitimately
100	// embed every forbidden token.
101	var selfPkgFragments = []string{
102		"/tools/lint/routinglint",
103		"tools/lint/routinglint",
104	}
105	
106	func inSelfPkg(pass *analysis.Pass) bool {
107		if pass.Pkg == nil {
108			return false
109		}
110		p := pass.Pkg.Path()
111		for _, f := range selfPkgFragments {
112			if strings.Contains(p, f) {
113				return true
114			}
115		}
116		return false
117	}
118	
119	func run(pass *analysis.Pass) (interface{}, error) {
120		if inSelfPkg(pass) {
121			return nil, nil
122		}
123	
124		insp := pass.ResultOf[inspect.Analyzer].(*inspector.Inspector)
125	
126		// Build a per-file index of lines that carry an exemption
127		// annotation, either as an inline comment on the same line or as
128		// a stand-alone comment line directly above. The annotation
129		// covers the line it sits on plus the line immediately following.
130		exempted := make(map[token.Position]bool)
131		for _, f := range pass.Files {
132			for _, cg := range f.Comments {
133				for _, c := range cg.List {
134					if !strings.Contains(c.Text, exemptionAnnotation) {
135						continue
136					}
137					if !exemptionRE.MatchString(c.Text) {
138						pass.Report(analysis.Diagnostic{
139							Pos:     c.Pos(),
140							Message: `routinglint: ` + exemptionAnnotation + ` annotation is missing a non-empty reason="..." clause`,
141						})
142						continue
143					}
144					cp := pass.Fset.Position(c.Pos())
145					exempted[token.Position{Filename: cp.Filename, Line: cp.Line}] = true
146					exempted[token.Position{Filename: cp.Filename, Line: cp.Line + 1}] = true
147				}
148			}
149		}
150	
151		isExempt := func(pos token.Pos) bool {
152			p := pass.Fset.Position(pos)
153			return exempted[token.Position{Filename: p.Filename, Line: p.Line}]
154		}
155	
156		report := func(pos token.Pos, msg string) {
157			if isExempt(pos) {
158				return
159			}
160			pass.Report(analysis.Diagnostic{Pos: pos, Message: msg})
161		}
162	
163		// Forbidden identifiers — flag at every occurrence, both
164		// declarations and references. Matching is exact on Ident.Name so
165		// substrings inside larger identifiers (e.g. the test name
166		// TestExecuteLoopLocalRejectsProfileLadders) do not trip the lint.
167		insp.Preorder([]ast.Node{(*ast.Ident)(nil)}, func(n ast.Node) {
168			id := n.(*ast.Ident)
169			if reason, ok := forbiddenIdents[id.Name]; ok {
170				report(id.Pos(), "routinglint: forbidden identifier "+id.Name+" — "+reason)
171			}
172		})
173	
174		// Forbidden string literals — flag the literal node itself.
175		insp.Preorder([]ast.Node{(*ast.BasicLit)(nil)}, func(n ast.Node) {
176			lit := n.(*ast.BasicLit)
177			if lit.Kind != token.STRING {
178				return
179			}
180			// Trim surrounding quote/backtick so we compare raw value.
181			s := lit.Value
182			if len(s) >= 2 && (s[0] == '"' || s[0] == '`') {
183				s = s[1 : len(s)-1]
184			}
185			if reason, ok := forbiddenLiterals[s]; ok {
186				report(lit.Pos(), "routinglint: forbidden string literal "+lit.Value+" — "+reason)
187			}
188		})
189	
190		return nil, nil
191	}
192

cli/.golangci.yml
version: "2"
run:
  tests: true
linters:
  enable:
    - errcheck
    - govet
    - ineffassign
    - staticcheck
  disable:
    - unused  # Many WIP functions, will clean up later
  settings:
    errcheck:
      check-blank: false  # Allow _ = assignment for intentional ignores
      exclude-functions:
        # Output functions where errors are typically ignored
        - 'fmt.Fprintf'
        - 'fmt.Fprintln'
        - 'fmt.Fprint'
        # Defer cleanup operations
        - '(*os.File).Close'
        # Cobra/pflag flag parsing (panics on error)
        - '(*github.com/spf13/pflag.FlagSet).GetBool'
        - '(*github.com/spf13/pflag.FlagSet).GetString'
        - '(*github.com/spf13/pflag.FlagSet).GetStringSlice'
        # Completion generation (best effort)
        - '(*github.com/spf13/cobra.Command).GenBashCompletion'
        - '(*github.com/spf13/cobra.Command).GenZshCompletion'
        - '(*github.com/spf13/cobra.Command).GenFishCompletion'
        - '(*github.com/spf13/cobra.Command).GenPowerShellCompletionWithDesc'
        # Color output (best effort)
        - '(*github.com/fatih/color.Color).Println'
        - '(*github.com/fatih/color.Color).Fprintf'
        - '(*github.com/fatih/color.Color).Fprintln'
        - '(*github.com/fatih/color.Color).Printf'
        # Cobra help output (best effort)
        - '(*github.com/spf13/cobra.Command).Help'
        # Test cleanup (intentional)
        - 'os.Remove'
        - 'os.Chmod'
(Bash completed with no output)

# Lefthook configuration for DDx project
# Fast, cross-platform git hooks
# https://github.com/evilmartians/lefthook

# Configuration
colors: true
no_tty: false
assertion: false

# Reusable command groups
command_groups:
  go_checks: &go_checks
    root: "cli/"  # Go module root
    tags: [go, backend]

  security_checks: &security_checks
    tags: [security]

  formatting_checks: &formatting_checks
    tags: [format, style]

# ====================
# Pre-commit hooks - Fast checks on staged files
# ====================
pre-commit:
  parallel: true

  commands:
    # Conflict detection using grep on staged files only.
    # Uses git diff --cached directly to avoid {staged_files} tty/expansion issues
    # when running detached (e.g. ddx work --local, no controlling tty).
    conflicts:
      name: "Check merge conflicts"
      run: |
        files=$(git diff --cached --name-only --diff-filter=ACMR 2>/dev/null)
        [ -z "$files" ] && exit 0
        conflict_file=$(printf '%s\n' "$files" | while IFS= read -r f; do
          [ -f "$f" ] || continue
          grep -lE "^<<<<<<<[ 	]|^=======$|^>>>>>>>[ 	]|^\|{7}[ 	]" "$f" 2>/dev/null && break
        done | head -1)
        if [ -n "$conflict_file" ]; then
          echo "Conflict markers found in: $conflict_file"
          exit 1
        fi
        exit 0
      fail_text: "Merge conflict markers found. Resolve conflicts before committing."

    # Debug statements - using ripgrep if available, fallback to grep
    # Note: Disabled because CLI applications legitimately use fmt.Print for output
    # debug-go:
    #   name: "Debug statements (Go)"
    #   glob: "*.go"
    #   exclude: "*_test.go"
    #   run: |
    #     if command -v rg >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    #       rg -q "(fmt\.Print|println|spew\.Dump|pprof\.|debug\.Print)" {staged_files} && exit 1 || exit 0
    #     else
    #       grep -E "(fmt\.Print|println|spew\.Dump|pprof\.|debug\.Print)" {staged_files} 2>/dev/null && exit 1 || exit 0
    #     fi
    #   fail_text: "Debug statements found in Go files"

    debug-python:
      name: "Debug statements (Python)"
      glob: "*.py"
      run: |
        if command -v rg >/dev/null 2>&1; then
          rg -q "(print\(|pdb\.set_trace|breakpoint\(\)|import pdb)" {staged_files} && exit 1 || exit 0
        else
          grep -E "(print\(|pdb\.set_trace|breakpoint\(\)|import pdb)" {staged_files} 2>/dev/null && exit 1 || exit 0

157	    # SD-024 Stage 4 runtime-lint: forbidden durable-knob fields on

159	    # Scoped to packages containing staged files, with diagnostics

162	    # gate in the `ci` block fires once the RunOptions/CompareOptions/
163	    # QuorumOptions retirement beads land.
164	    runtime-lint:
165	      <<: *go_checks
166	      name: "Runtime Lint (SD-024 forbidden runtime fields + legacy options)"
167	      glob: "cli/**/*.go"
168	      run: |
169	        staged=$(git diff --cached --name-only --diff-filter=ACMR | grep -E '^cli/.+\.go$' || true)
170	        [ -z "$staged" ] && exit 0
171	        pkgs=$(printf '%s\n' "$staged" | xargs -n1 dirname | sort -u | sed 's|^cli/|./|' | grep -v '^\./tools/lint/runtimelint' || true)
172	        [ -z "$pkgs" ] && exit 0
173	        out=$(go run ./tools/lint/runtimelint/cmd/runtimelint $pkgs 2>&1) || true
174	        violations=""
175	        for f in $staged; do

180	          fi
181	        done
182	        if [ -n "$violations" ]; then
183	          printf '%s' "$violations"
184	          exit 1
185	        fi
186	        exit 0

189	        - rebase

Error: unknown flag: --version
Usage:
  ddx [command]

work drains the execution-ready bead queue. It is the FEAT-010 layer-3
queue drain: it iterates ddx try (layer 2) across ready beads until a stop
condition is met and owns retry-power policy between attempts.

Unlike "ddx agent execute-loop", ddx work treats --harness, --provider, and
--model as opaque passthrough constraints forwarded to the agent unchanged.
DDx does not validate these values or branch on them; the agent owns routing
within the requested power bounds.

Stop conditions (evaluated between attempts):
  drained     — no ready beads remain
  blocked     — every remaining bead has produced a terminal non-success outcome
  deferred    — configured budget exhausted
  no_progress — N consecutive attempts produced no commit (default N=3)
  signal      — SIGINT/SIGTERM received between attempts

By default work submits to the running ddx server as a background worker and
returns immediately. Use --local to run inline in the current process.

Usage:
  ddx work [flags]

Examples:
  # Drain the current execution-ready queue once and exit
  ddx work

  # Pick one ready bead, execute it, and stop
  ddx work --local --once

  # Run continuously as a bounded queue worker
  ddx work --poll-interval 30s

  # Forward harness/model as passthrough constraints (ddx does not validate these)
  ddx work --once --harness agent --model minimax/minimax-m2.7

  # Constrain power tier (retry-power policy is owned by ddx work)
  ddx work --once --min-power 40 --max-power 90

Flags:
      --effort string              Effort level
      --from string                Base git revision to start from (default: HEAD)
      --harness string             Agent harness constraint (passthrough; ddx work does not validate)
  -h, --help                       help for work
      --json                       Output loop result as JSON
      --local                      Run inline in current process instead of server worker (default: submit to server)
      --max-cost float             Stop when accumulated billed cost exceeds USD; 0 = unlimited (default 100)
      --max-power int              Maximum model power allowed (0 = unconstrained); passed to agent routing unchanged
      --min-power int              Minimum model power required (0 = unconstrained); passed to agent routing unchanged
      --model string               Model constraint (passthrough; ddx work does not validate)
      --model-ref string           Model catalog reference (e.g. code-medium); resolved via the model catalog

data.text:
Commit `26c089e0` lands cleanly on top of base `ed4415eb`. All four ACs satisfied:

- **AC#1**: `.agents/skills/cli/` and `.claude/skills/cli/` (538 files each, 1077 total) deleted.
- **AC#2**: `routinglint` wired into `cli/Makefile` `lint` target; probe verified `make lint` fails on `AdaptiveMinTier` reintroduction with clear diagnostic.
- **AC#3**: Evidence in `.ddx/executions/20260502T194248-7843b153/verification.txt` — alpha7 binary `strings` shows zero `adaptive.min` hits; worker spawn output contains no "adaptive min-tier" string.
- **AC#4**: `grep -rn 'adaptive.min.tier\|AdaptiveMinTier\|--no-adaptive\|--adaptive-min-tier-window' cli/` → 3 hits, all inside the routinglint analyzer's own closed-list and testdata fixture (self-exempt per `selfPkgFragments`).

Note: while running the `ddx work --once` verification probe, an out-of-band concurrent execute-bead session was triggered; I soft-reset to base and re-staged only the bead-scoped changes. The unstaged `.ddx/beads.jsonl` modification and the `.ddx/executions/20260502T194621-5375ba92/` directory belong to that other attempt and were left untouched.
